So right now when you're going through these portals go through one and execute it and go through the next one and execute it. Okay, like don't try and do everything at once. And that's just with this portal. But also I can't tell you in a lot of times where I'll cover two different things. One is where I'll have a gym owner who's like struggling and they're like, well, I've got a meal prep business and I'm also doing an online business and like they're not making any money on any of them. Right, it's because the thought process is, well, I'm going to start all of them and one of them will take off. But that's not how business works. Any one of them can be a successful business, but you are the one who's going to make it successful by investing. Right. And so you have to start like prune the tree, cut off the branches that are going to die so you can make the tree trunk grow. Right. So only one opportunity. And then if you are someone who has multiple locations right now and I can't tell you how many it's like one, you're not alone in that a lot of people when they grow their locations, their actual net profit goes down. So if that is you, it's okay. You didn't know. Now you know. Consider downsizing. I've walked a zillion gym owners through downsizing locations so that they can recapture attention and then they downsize, they go from three relations to one and their net profit triples. Right. Cause then everything gets really easy. They're focusing on this one thing and then now they have the systems to automate their business so they can actually have one that runs extremely profitably and they don't have to be there. Right. Like you can make 20, 30, $40,000 a month net from a gym and not be there. You don't need to have, if you're making 10,000 a month at one gym and then you open a second because you're trying to get 20, you can just make 20 from one. You don't need to open a second gym to do that. Right. And I have metrics around what, what barriers you need to hit or what benchmarks you need to hit to open the second location. I won't get into those now, but point is if you have multiple locations, consider downsizing. If you're having more than one opportunity, cut them. And then if you're going through these portals, go through one at a time, execute, go to the next. Do less and say no more. Meaning a lot of people are going to ask for you to do things and the more successful we become, the bigger the opportunities you have to turn down. I can't tell you the size of the opportunities that we have to turn down on a regular basis, but the better, like you will get better at saying no. And it will get to the point where your impulse will be to say no. Sometimes I say no, and I'm not even thinking about what they asked me because I just know that nine times out of 10, it should be no. It's just they're like some guy from work that you're not that. Well, that's not a good example here, but there are people who will ask you to do things. Can you help me move? Right? Is this relationship something that I really, really want to invest in? I don't know. You have to like, you have to use discernment with these things, but most times say no and then focus on what you need to do. Drinking. I'm not saying you can't drink. That's not my, I'm not your, I'm not your czar. You know what I mean? But I will say that when we stop drinking, our life got a lot better. And you don't need to stop. I would say stop getting drunk. Because ultimately when you stop getting drunk, then you stop masking the issues that you're trying to cover. And then it forces you to confront the things that you're running from. And I'm not that, like you're running from stuff, but when you are stressed at the end of the day, you feel anxiety and it feels good to have a couple beers or like some scotch or whatever it is or whatever your vodka, whatever your thing is, right? That's the moment where you should be thinking, how can I make myself less anxious? How can I handle these issues rather than drinking this to feel better? Because there will come a time and what happens is, and this is the crappy part, is that when you stop drinking, life will get louder. So temporarily things will get louder, things will get harder, more things will be taking your attention, but then you handle one at a time. And then by the end of that cycle, you'll have all your attention back and you won't need the drink to feel relaxed. Okay? So I would encourage you, at least for X period of time, do it for a year. Just don't drink for a year. Not forever. Just don't drink for a year and see how much you get back in your attention about the things you handle, the relationships you improve because you're never making mistakes with that kind of stuff. And you'll see what friends you really have. If your friends don't want to go out with you, if you're not drinking, then they're probably not the best friends. Right? And if you can't have fun without drinking, like for real though, that's probably an issue. Like you really probably need to handle that. Like you're not as happy the two to three days after significant drinking. Like you're not.
